tasks.design: Clarification (interview 2026-06-22): ROOT CAUSE — fetch.ts persists only innerText (pages/<id>.txt); the accord bar widths live in raw HTML that is saved ONLY for failed pages (pages-failed/<id>.html). T009's parser reads pages/<id>.html, which never exists on the success path, so pct always falls back to null. FIX: extend fetch.ts to ALSO persist the raw HTML alongside the .txt so widths survive going forward (keep the .txt for the Claude parse step). The committed unit test stays hermetic on a synthetic HTML fixture (do NOT commit real Fragrantica HTML). END-TO-END proof is for the agent's own confidence: ONE local live fetch of a single perfume (paced via the fragrantica service) is allowed to confirm fetch.ts persists HTML and parse fills real pcts — be frugal; the monthly cap is the only hard ceiling. The existing 42 outputs stay null until re-fetched in production.

tasks.design: Model directly on src/services/plex.service.ts (the confirmed reference pattern): default-export a ServiceDefinition named 'fs' with paid: false and NO ratePerMinute/dailyCap/monthlyCap/minIntervalMs/maxJitterMs — there is no real external rate limit to model, only the owner's own local disk. description must explain this exists purely so the inputKeysService contract (a later unit) can require EVERY inputKeys()-declaring job to name a real registered service, with zero carve-outs for 'this one just reads a local file' — i.e. this is a deliberate conceptual stretch chosen by the owner to keep the enforcement rule mechanically universal, not because there's a real quota/rate limit to govern. rateLimitSource should say something like 'Local disk the owner controls — no external rate limit or quota applies; this service exists purely so a local-file-backed inputKeys() can be governed by the same callService contract as every real external dependency.' category: the current controlled vocabulary (checked live: src/services/*.service.ts + root CLAUDE.md's T305 note) is only 'cli-tool' | 'website-scrape' | 'api', with 'uncategorized' as the unset default — none fit 'local filesystem' cleanly. Introduce a new category value 'local' (used by exactly this one service for now) rather than force-fitting into 'api' — but this is a judgment call the builder should make explicitly and document (either choice is acceptable; do not silently default to uncategorized without a documented reason). No dashboard edit UI is needed for category (per the existing T292/T305 convention — category is manifest-owned only). timeoutMs: use a short default (e.g. a few seconds) reflecting that a local file read is near-instant, rather than reusing a network-service-scale timeout default.

tasks.title: Migrate places' resolveInputKeys() off the self-referential data/out/places.json read onto the fs service + shared CSV-parse helper
tasks.design: Current broken state (verified live, src/workflows/places/stages/resolve.ts:18-26): resolveInputKeys() reads placesConfig.placesOut (data/out/places.json), which is written by the UPSTREAM places-ingest stage on a prior run — after a 'Clear output data' reset this returns [] until an unlimited run reseeds the file, which is exactly the trap idea 13 describes (the T094 selectPendingRoots empty-candidate-list -> ctx.rootAllowed rejects everything including the root stage's own recording -> silent no-op). Fix: change resolveInputKeys() to derive the candidate CID list DIRECTLY from the raw Google Takeout CSVs in data/raw/Saved/ — the SAME true source places-ingest itself parses (verified: src/workflows/places/stages/ingest.ts's runIngest() already does readdirSync(placesConfig.savedDir) + parseListFile()+extractFeatureId() from ../parse.js to build exactly this CID list) — routed through callService('fs', () => <csv read + parse>). Avoid duplicating the CSV-parsing/CID-extraction logic a second time: extract the minimal shared piece (walk data/raw/Saved/*.csv, parse each with the existing parseListFile, pull cid via extractFeatureId, dedupe) into a small exported helper (e.g. in ingest.ts or parse.ts) that BOTH runIngest() and resolveInputKeys() call, so the two can never drift out of sync. Keep runIngest()'s own behavior/output unchanged (still writes places.json + validation-report.json + records the ledger) — only its internal CID-list-building step should now call the shared helper instead of (or alongside) its existing inline loop, whichever refactor is cleanest without changing runIngest's logged narration or validation-report semantics. Update places/CLAUDE.md's cid-to-place-id-resolver section to note resolveInputKeys() now derives from the raw CSVs via the fs service, not from places.json.

tasks.title: Fix plex-language-fix's discoverInputKeys() self-referential ledger read with a live Plex library walk
tasks.design: Current broken state (verified live, src/workflows/plex-language-fix/stages/discover.ts:23-25): discoverInputKeys() calls ledgerSuccessRows(JOB_NAME) — reads back THIS SAME JOB's own prior work_items 'success' rows (stages/ledger.ts), which is exactly the self-referential trap: after a ledger reset it returns [], selectedRoots becomes empty, and ctx.rootAllowed rejects even the root stage's own fresh discoveries (see discover.ts:89's recordFile()'s `if (!ctx.rootAllowed(key)) return;` check on the SAME stage that's supposed to be populating the ledger). Fix: change discoverInputKeys() to perform a live walk of the configured Plex library sections (mirroring plex-profiles' resolveInputKeys() in src/workflows/plex-profiles/stages/build.ts:41-51, and reusing runDiscover()'s own existing fetchSections/fetchSectionItems fetch logic in this same file rather than re-implementing it) and return the resulting file keys (fileKey(itemRatingKey, partId) for every movie/episode part found) computed fresh each call — a live query against the true external source (Plex), never this workflow's own ledger. This is a genuinely more expensive candidate-enumeration than before (a full or partial library crawl per manual limited-run request) — this is the KNOWN, EXPLICITLY ACCEPTED tradeoff named in idea 13's text; do not try to avoid it by caching. Per the sequencing decision, this depends on T474 (which migrates this workflow's plexGet-based calls, including this file's fetchSections/fetchSectionItems machinery, onto callService('plex', ...)) landing first — after T474, whatever discoverInputKeys() calls to enumerate the library should already be routed through the plex service by construction (reusing the now-wrapped fetch functions), giving this unit its inputKeysService:'plex' backing for free rather than needing to add a second, parallel callService('plex', ...) wrap. If T474 has NOT landed by the time this unit is picked, the builder must still wrap the live Plex calls this unit adds in callService('plex', ...) directly (don't leave a raw plexGet call), so the dependency is a strong recommendation for ordering/reuse, not a hard technical blocker. Update plex-language-fix/CLAUDE.md's discover-stage description to reflect the new live-walk-based inputKeys() (replacing the 'Returns every file key the discover ledger already knows about' language) and to note the per-limited-run crawl-cost tradeoff.

tasks.title: Fix projects-sync's githubSyncInputKeys() self-referential catalog-file read with a live GitHub API call

A full multi-page fetch for inputKeys() alone may be wasteful just to enumerate candidate ids; consider whether a single-page fetch (or the full fetchAllRepos()) is more appropriate given inputKeys() is only called once per manual limited-run start, not per scheduled run — builder's call, document the choice. Update projects-sync/CLAUDE.md's github-sync stage description to note inputKeys() now queries GitHub live rather than reading back the catalog file.

Retrofit onto every job in the registry that currently declares inputKeys() (5 total, confirmed by exploration): (1) perfumes' find-url.job.ts -> inputKeysService: 'dynamodb' (already live via loadPerfumes()'s callService('dynamodb', ...) — pure field addition, no behavior change); (2) places' resolve.ts's job definition (wherever the JobDefinition with inputKeys: resolveInputKeys is assembled — locate the *.job.ts that wires this in) -> inputKeysService: 'fs' (now true after unit B); (3) plex-language-fix's discover job definition -> inputKeysService: 'plex' (now true after unit C, which depends on T474); (4) projects-sync's github-sync job definition -> inputKeysService: 'github' (now true after unit D); (5) plex-profiles' build job definition -> inputKeysService: 'plex' (true once T474 lands, since T474's scope already includes src/workflows/plex-profiles/stages/build.ts's plexGet calls — confirm at build time that resolveInputKeys() itself, not just runBuild(), is wrapped in callService('plex', ...) per T474's Done-when; if T474 wrapped only the main build loop's plexGet calls and NOT resolveInputKeys()'s two plexGet calls, this unit must add the callService('plex', ...) wrap to resolveInputKeys() itself as a small supplementary fix, since the inputKeysService claim must be truthful). Each workflow's own CLAUDE.md should get a one-line note that its root stage's inputKeys() now declares inputKeysService alongside the existing description. Do NOT touch the enforcing test (next unit) or make any further behavioral changes to inputKeys() itself here — this unit is scoped to the field + the 5 retrofits only.

tasks.title: Add mechanical test enforcing every inputKeys()-declaring job also names a valid inputKeysService
tasks.design: Mirror src/core/gate-coverage.test.ts's existing walk-the-registry pattern exactly (same imports from src/workflows/registry.js, same lightweight test()/assert helper style, same 'real-registry walk' + small synthetic pass/fail regression-guard tests shape). New test file src/core/root-inputkeys-service.test.ts: (1) load every job the registry discovers (jobs from src/workflows/registry.js); (2) for every job with a truthy `inputKeys` field, assert `inputKeysService` is also set to a non-empty string, failing with a clear message naming the offending job if not; (3) assert that string matches the `name` of a real ServiceDefinition the registry loaded (services from the same registry module — confirm the registry's exported service list name/shape before wiring this check); (4) include the same style of small synthetic positive/negative regression-guard tests gate-coverage.test.ts has (a fake job with inputKeys+valid inputKeysService passes; a fake job with inputKeys but no/invalid inputKeysService is genuinely caught) so the checker itself is tested, not just exercised once against current registry state. This test MUST NOT be added before units A-E (and transitively T474) have landed, since it will otherwise immediately fail npm test against the current codebase (places/plex-language-fix/projects-sync currently have inputKeys with no inputKeysService at all) — the dependsOn on idea-13-17-e (which itself depends on a-d and T474) enforces this ordering. Typecheck + test scripts/, and fix the three test-runner defects
tasks.design: scripts/*.ts is currently invisible to both tsc and the test runner: tsconfig `include` is only ["src/**/*.ts"] (tsconfig.json:15) and run-tests.ts walks only 'src' (scripts/run-tests.ts:30). So `npx tsc --noEmit` never typechecks the 7 scripts/*.ts files — including plex-language-undo.ts, the safety-net revert tool for the one workflow that mutates the owner's Plex server — and scripts/plex-language-undo.test.ts is a dead test (passes when run manually, never discovered by `npm test`). Fix: (a) tsconfig include becomes ["src/**/*.ts","scripts/**/*.ts"] (keep the existing exclude of node_modules/dashboard/src-data); (b) run-tests.ts walks [...walk('src'), ...walk('scripts')] (scripts already use the NodeNext import idiom; the undo test passes today, so low-risk). The run-tests.ts walk() change here is the BASE that CI-TEST-02 (dashboard suites) builds on — do this first. THREE runner defects to also fix in run-tests.ts (defect analysis in idea T02): (1) verdict ordering — the runner prints its synchronous '✓ all tests passed' banner BEFORE node:test's describe/it drain (~10+ files use node:test, which runs after the serial imports resolve), so the last banner can misreport even though the exit code is correct; move the final PASS/FAIL verdict into a process.on('beforeExit') hook (or run each file as a child process, see (2)) so it lands after the drain. (2) no timeout anywhere — one hanging it() or top-level await hangs npm test/CI until the 6h Actions kill; add a per-file wall-clock timeout. The idea's preferred shape is running each test file as a child process with a per-file timeout (also buys isolation — today 174 files share one process + one scratch DB — and parallelism), but a simpler per-file timeout that keeps the single-process model is acceptable if child-process isolation proves too invasive for this unit. (3) scratch-DB collision — the npm `test` script hardcodes `rm -f /tmp/lj-test.db*; LOCALJOBS_DB=/tmp/lj-test.db …` (package.json:15), so two concurrent `npm test` runs (a manual run alongside the harness loop's DoD check — a real scenario documented in CLAUDE.md) share and mutually delete each other's live DB; drop LOCALJOBS_DB (and the rm) from the npm script and let config.ts's isTestEnv/resolveDbPath mint the per-PID scratch path it already unit-tests (or use mktemp -d). Keep the suite's current green state as the regression bar. NOTE the shared scratch-DB fix interacts with any child-process isolation from defect (2) — each worker still needs its own per-PID scratch DB.
tasks.verify: `npx tsc --noEmit` typechecks scripts/*.ts (deliberately breaking plex-language-undo.ts's imports fails tsc). `npm test` discovers and runs scripts/plex-language-undo.test.ts. A deliberately-failing node:test file makes the runner's LAST printed line the failure verdict. A deliberately-hanging test file fails within the per-file timeout, not the CI kill. Two concurrent `npm test` invocations both pass (no shared-DB deletion). All existing tests stay green.

tasks.title: Pin the Node major across all three environments (engines + .nvmrc)
tasks.design: Node is pinned only in CI (setup-node node-version: 22, ci.yml:22). Local happens to match and the launchd plists bake a node path at install time — three environments can silently diverge. This is the SHARED Node-pinning deliverable that both the CI-hardening task (CI-TEST-04) and the launchd-installer task (CI-TEST-05) reference. Add `"engines": { "node": ">=22 <23" }` to BOTH package.json and dashboard/package.json, and create a repo-root `.nvmrc` containing `22`. Keep the CI setup-node value (22) consistent with the pin. Note: `engines` is advisory by default under npm (npm only warns, not refuses, unless engine-strict is set) — that matches the idea's 'warns/refuses per engines' acceptance; do not add engine-strict enforcement unless the owner asks.

tasks.title: CI hardening: dashboard npm cache path + npm audit for both roots
tasks.design: CI correctly runs the documented DoD (tsc --noEmit, npm test, dashboard npm ci + build, with concurrency-cancel). Two remaining gaps: (1) setup-node's `cache: npm` keys on the ROOT lockfile only (ci.yml:20-23), so the dashboard's `npm --prefix dashboard ci` re-downloads its entire tree every run — fix with `cache-dependency-path: ['package-lock.json', 'dashboard/package-lock.json']` on the setup-node step. (2) No npm audit anywhere — for a LAN-exposed always-on web server, staying current matters; add an `npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high` step for BOTH package roots (root and dashboard). Whether that step BLOCKS CI (fails the build) or merely WARNS/annotates is an explicit OWNER decision — see pending-questions (topic:other); do NOT default silently. Implement per the owner's answer: blocking = let the audit step's non-zero exit fail the job; non-blocking = append `|| true` (or a continue-on-error step) so it surfaces in the log without failing. This unit depends on CI-TEST-03 so the setup-node Node pin and the cache-path change land coherently on the same step. If both dashboard/package-lock.json referenced by the cache path and the audit assume a dashboard lockfile exists, confirm dashboard/package-lock.json is committed (it must be for `npm ci` to work today).
tasks.verify: The CI dashboard-install step hits the npm cache on unchanged lockfiles (cache-dependency-path lists both lockfiles). An `npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high` step runs for both roots and behaves per the owner's blocking-vs-warn decision. CI stays green on the current clean tree (assuming no current high advisory, or non-blocking mode).

tasks.title: launchd installer hygiene: resolve node at run time, drop baked plist path, fix stale 3001 comment
tasks.design: The launchd installers freeze the nvm-versioned node path into the plist at install time via `NODE_BIN_DIR="$(dirname "$(command -v node)")"` (install-launchd.sh:8,31-36; install-dashboard-launchd.sh:9,30-39). After `nvm install <newer>` the daemon silently keeps running old Node; after uninstalling that version the daemon DIES at boot with a PATH error visible only in daemon.err.log. Fix: resolve the node bin dir at RUN time inside start-daemon.sh (which already owns PATH injection at start-daemon.sh:9-12) instead of baking it into the plist — the plist then only needs the wrapper path. Apply the same run-time-resolution shape to the dashboard agent (whose plist directly invokes `$NODE_BIN_DIR/npm` at install-dashboard-launchd.sh:30 — route it through a wrapper or otherwise resolve node at run time so no frozen path is baked in). Practical run-time resolution: source nvm (or read .nvmrc — CI-TEST-03 lands it) and/or probe common nvm/homebrew node locations, falling back to PATH. Pair with the Node pin (CI-TEST-03, dependsOn) so all three environments agree on the Node major. Also FIX the stale header comment in install-dashboard-launchd.sh:2-3 (`http://localhost:3001` → 4788; the final echo at line 58 is already correct). Optional modernization: move `launchctl load/unload` (install-launchd.sh:57-58, install-dashboard-launchd.sh:56-57) to `bootstrap/bootout gui/$(id -u)` for better error messages — IN vs OUT of scope is an open owner question; default to leaving load/unload unless the owner says otherwise. Acceptance is manual-only on the Mini (installer scripts have no test harness): install → verify plist contents no longer bake a node path → switch nvm default → `launchctl kickstart -k` → /api/health responds; removing the old nvm version does not kill the daemon at boot; installers re-run cleanly over an existing install.
tasks.verify: Manual on the Mini (no test harness): after `nvm install <new>` + `launchctl kickstart -k`, the daemon picks up the new Node WITHOUT re-running the installer; removing the old nvm version does not kill the daemon at boot; both installers re-run cleanly over an existing install; the generated plists no longer contain a frozen nvm node path. The install-dashboard-launchd.sh header comment reads 4788, not 3001.

tasks.title: Add a sequence token + visibility poll-pause to usePoll (out-of-order + hidden-tab)
tasks.design: Q04 items 1 + 4. usePoll (ui.tsx:282-310): an interval tick and a manual refetch(), or a slow prior tick when latency > interval (plausible on the 1s run page), can resolve out of order and the OLDER response wins setData. Add a sequence token: const seq = ++seqRef.current captured before the await; apply setData/setError only if seq is still current (seqRef.current === seq). Also add intervalMs to the effect deps array (currently only `deps` is passed) so a dynamic interval would actually re-apply — latent today but correct. Pure hook logic — NOT a visible surface change, so leave visualVerify unset. usePoll is exported from ui.tsx, so a pure hook test can drive it: assert that when a newer tick resolves before an older one, the older response never overwrites setData.
tasks.verify: npx tsc --noEmit, npm test (the new pure hook test for the sequence token — older response after newer never overwrites), npm --prefix dashboard run build.

tasks.title: Add an hours tier to fmtDuration and NaN/skew guards to fmtRelative
tasks.design: Q05 items 1 + 2. (1) fmtDuration (ui.tsx:64-71) has no hours tier — a 2h5m run renders '125m 12s'; the monthly recommender workflows plausibly exceed an hour. Add an 'Xh Ym' tier above 60 minutes. (2) fmtRelative (ui.tsx:80-91) lacks the already-ISO guard that fmtTime (:73-78) has ('…Z' + 'Z' -> NaN -> 'NaNs ago' if ever fed an ISO string) and shows '-2s ago' under clock skew. tasks.title: Add defineService() + envInt() to src/services/lib.ts (validated env reads, monthly/30 sentinel, required rateLimitSource)
tasks.design: Add two helpers to src/services/lib.ts. (1) envInt(varName: string, fallback: number): number -- reads process.env[varName], and if set, parses it strictly (reject empty string, non-finite, NaN, and negative values) THROWING an Error whose message NAMES the offending var (e.g. `env var VERCEL_MONTHLY_CAP is not a valid non-negative integer: "2,000"`) so a poisoned env fails LOUD at module load; when unset, returns fallback. This is the services-side implementation of the separate B19 proposal (unvalidated Number(process.env...) reads) -- bundled here per the R04 idea. (2) defineService(d): ServiceDefinition -- a declarative factory taking { name; category; description; paid; rateLimitSource (REQUIRED -- so a missing one, e.g. vercel's today, is a TYPE error); envPrefix?; ratePerMinute?; dailyCap?: number|'monthly/30'; monthlyCap?; minIntervalMs?; maxJitterMs?; timeoutMs?; cacheTtlMs? } and returning a ServiceDefinition. It resolves each numeric field through envInt using the envPrefix convention (e.g. envPrefix:'FINNHUB' -> FINNHUB_RATE_PER_MIN / FINNHUB_DAILY_CAP / FINNHUB_MONTHLY_CAP / FINNHUB_MIN_INTERVAL_MS / FINNHUB_MAX_JITTER_MS / FINNHUB_TIMEOUT_MS / FINNHUB_CACHE_TTL_MS) but MUST allow a service to keep its existing bespoke env var names (e.g. gemini's PLACES_LLM_MONTHLY_CAP, fragrantica's PERFUMES_FETCH_DELAY_MS) so effective limits are byte-identical to today -- support passing an explicit resolved number OR an explicit envVarName override per field. The dailyCap:'monthly/30' sentinel resolves to dailyFromMonthly(resolvedMonthlyCap) (with envInt applied to the daily override env var first, matching today's `?? dailyFromMonthly(monthlyCap)` pattern). Design defineService so it can also express the plex service shape in flight (T511-T517) -- i.e. don't hardcode assumptions that break minIntervalMs-only or timeoutMs-only services. Do NOT migrate any service in this unit -- only add + unit-test the helper. types.ts is in scope ONLY if defineService needs a small exported input-type addition; the ServiceDefinition interface itself is unchanged.
tasks.verify: npx tsc --noEmit; npm test with a new src/services/lib.test.ts asserting: (a) envInt returns fallback when unset, parses a valid int when set, and THROWS naming the var on empty-string / '2,000' / NaN / negative; (b) defineService produces a ServiceDefinition with the right resolved numeric fields via envPrefix and via bespoke-env-name overrides; (c) the 'monthly/30' dailyCap sentinel resolves to dailyFromMonthly(monthlyCap); (d) omitting rateLimitSource is a compile error (documented in the test file as a // @ts-expect-error case).

tasks.title: Treat Claude rate-limit as a soft pause/break, not a hard failure (3 jobs)
tasks.design: In three jobs, `if (!result.ok) throw` collapses a Claude `result.rateLimited === true` outcome into a generic failure. Consequence: the job burns its maxRetries against a usage limit that won't clear in minutes, and project-summarize keeps hammering every remaining repo after the first rate-limited one. The claude helper (src/services/claude.ts) already exposes result.rateLimited; perfumes/movies/tv-recs already treat it as a soft pause (see movies/stages/recommend.ts ~155: warn + skip, run continues). Fix each: BEFORE the generic throw, check result.rateLimited. For the SINGLE-ARTIFACT stages (workouts-progress.ts ~247-249, stock-digest-build.ts ~271-273): log a warn ('Claude rate/usage limit hit — pausing, will retry next run') and soft-return (leave the item un-done so a later run resumes) instead of throwing. For project-summarize.ts (a per-repo loop, ~244-246): on rateLimited, break the loop (stop processing remaining repos this run) rather than throwing/continuing — leaving unprocessed repos for the next run. Non-rate-limit failures (result.ok false but rateLimited false) keep their existing throw/failed behaviour. In project-summarize, be careful not to trip the T416 'fail run if any item failed' rule for a rateLimited break: a rate-limit pause is a soft-stop (like a quota skip), NOT a per-item 'failed', so it must not mark the current repo 'failed' and must not push the run into the failed tally.
tasks.verify: Add a unit test per job: inject a claude runner returning { ok:false, rateLimited:true }; assert the single-artifact stages soft-return (no throw, no 'failed' ledger row, item left un-done) and project-summarize breaks the loop (remaining repos untouched, no run-level failure thrown for the rate-limit alone). Keep a companion assertion that a non-rate-limit { ok:false, rateLimited:false } still fails as before. Existing tests green; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

tasks.title: plex-language-fix: real evaluate->apply gate before the only externally-mutating stage
tasks.design: plex-language-fix's three gate contracts (contracts.ts ~37/54/71) all return ok:true unconditionally — a sanctioned trivial minimum, BUT this DAG ends in the repo's ONLY externally-mutating stage (apply.ts PUTs audio/subtitle track selection to Plex). The evaluate->apply gate (plexLanguageEvaluateContract) can cheaply assert something real, catching evaluate-logic drift BEFORE mutation: 'every evaluate work_items row with detail.status === "change" has a numeric proposedAudio.streamId AND a non-null currentAudio' — exactly the malformation apply.ts (~53-56) currently skips silently at runtime (`!discover || typeof evalDetail.proposedAudio?.streamId !== 'number'`). ~15 lines, high leverage. Read the evaluate rows read-only via stages/ledger.ts's ledgerSuccessRows (already used by apply.ts) — never a paid/remote call, keeping the contract check() cheap + side-effect-free per the gate-page-safety rule. Keep the check()'s per-expectation checks[]/sample shape via the existing fromChecks helper so the gate detail page still renders; an all-'skip' or empty ledger passes trivially (no 'change' rows to violate). On violation, list the offending itemKey(s) in the checks[].actual/sample so the gate page names exactly which evaluate row is malformed. RELATES to pending Plex-migration task T511 (migrating plex-language-fix onto callService('plex')) — note only, do NOT hard-depend; this gate is on the evaluate ledger shape, orthogonal to how apply reaches Plex. The other two contracts (discover, resolve) stay trivial — only the evaluate->apply boundary gets the real assertion.

Retrofit onto every job in the registry that currently declares inputKeys() — CONFIRMED this session, exactly 5, and the field goes on the JobDefinition object, which lives in the *.job.ts file, NOT any sibling non-.job.ts impl file of a similar name: (1) src/workflows/perfumes/stages/find-url.job.ts -> inputKeysService: 'dynamodb' (already live via loadPerfumes()'s callService('dynamodb', ...) — pure field addition, no behavior change); (2) src/workflows/places/stages/resolve.job.ts -> inputKeysService: 'fs' (now true after T484 — do NOT touch src/workflows/places/stages/resolve.ts, the impl file, unless the JobDefinition wiring genuinely requires it); (3) src/workflows/plex-language-fix/stages/discover.job.ts -> inputKeysService: 'plex' (now true after T485 + T474/T578); (4) src/workflows/projects-sync/stages/github-sync.job.ts -> inputKeysService: 'github' (now true after T486); (5) src/workflows/plex-profiles/stages/build.job.ts -> inputKeysService: 'plex' (true once T474/T578 landed — confirm at build time that resolveInputKeys() itself, not just runBuild(), is wrapped in callService('plex', ...); if not, this unit must add the callService('plex', ...) wrap to resolveInputKeys() itself as a small supplementary fix, since the inputKeysService claim must be truthful). ALL FIVE touched workflows' own CLAUDE.md (perfumes, places, plex-language-fix, projects-sync, plex-profiles) get a one-line note that its root stage's inputKeys() now declares inputKeysService alongside the existing description, plus the root src/workflows/CLAUDE.md documents the new field and its enforcement in the root-stage-ledger convention section. Do NOT touch the enforcing test (that is T488, a separate task) or make any further behavioral changes to inputKeys() itself here beyond the supplementary plex-profiles callService wrap noted above if needed.

tasks.title: Collapse the four managers into a generic RecsManager + GroupedManager (re-attempt of T543)
tasks.design: Building on R05-a's extracted files: TvRecsManager and MovieRecsManager are ~95% identical (167 lines each) — differences are only the API method names (tvRecs/ignoreTvRec/unignoreTvRec/unignoreTvRecBulk vs the movie equivalents), the TMDB URL segment (/tv/ vs /movie/), the noun (show vs film), and the heading. Collapse them into ONE generic RecsManager<T> parameterized by { heading, noun, tmdbPath: 'tv'|'movie', fetch, ignore, unignore, unignoreBulk }. Similarly MovieGapsManager (~205) and MissingSeasonsManager (~204) share the grouped-table shape (group header + Ignore-all/Un-ignore-all + per-item buttons + IgnoredSection, ~80% column overlap) — collapse into ONE generic GroupedManager<T> parameterized by { groupBy, itemKey, columns, and the group's ignore/unignore/ignore-bulk API fns }. All four call-sites become thin config objects. Reuse the existing SortTh, IgnoredSection, Pill, and useAction (from R05-a). Must be pixel-equivalent. Also update CLAUDE.md's 'Shared dashboard UI components' section (the entries naming TvRecsManager.tsx/MovieRecsManager.tsx/MovieGapsManager.tsx/MissingSeasonsManager.tsx by file path, and the two other prose mentions of these four names elsewhere in the file) to describe the two new generic components instead — this is now explicitly in scope.
